relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks holiness boundaries.
The foundation remains: I am YHWH your Divine.
Israel is not to copy Egypt or Canaan as identity pattern.
YHWH's judgments and statutes shape the walk.
The statutes are for life before YHWH.
Kinship closeness is not to become sexual violation.
The mother's nakedness is guarded.
The father's household boundary is guarded.
The sister's nakedness is guarded.
Descendant boundaries are guarded.
The sister born in the father's house is guarded.
The father's sister is guarded.
The mother's sister is guarded.
The uncle's wife is guarded.
The daughter-in-law is guarded.
The brother's wife is guarded.
A woman and her descendants are not to be bound into one violating arrangement.
Sisters are not to be made rivals through sexual taking.
Menstrual separation is guarded as ritual boundary.
The companion's marriage is guarded from betrayal.
Children are not to be given to Molech; the Divine name is not profaned.
The male-male boundary text is preserved for deep source and reception audit, never for hate.
Human-animal boundary is guarded from violation.
Israel is warned not to be formed by these boundary violations.
The land itself is pictured as reacting to accumulated iniquity.
Native and sojourner alike are held to the land's holiness boundaries.
The prior inhabitants are described as making the land unclean.
Israel is warned not to repeat land-defiling patterns.
Boundary-breaking brings covenant cutting-off language.
The chapter closes with YHWH's charge and name.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 18 is a difficult boundary text. Its safest mystical handling is not hate, but vigilance against violation, coercion, kinship harm, child-sacrifice, betrayal, and any use of Scripture to dehumanize people.
